LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF RONALD WILSON'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS COUNSEL S. ASHAR AHMED AND OR NOSSAMAN LLP IN THE AMOUNT OF $6,446.00. - MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHALL PROVIDE VERIFIED AMENDED RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 1-4, 7-10, 13-15, 18-20, 29-32, 35-39, 43-50, 52-60, 61-64. REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. DEFENSE COUNSEL IS REMINDED TO COMPLY WITH CRC 8.1115(A). PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS REMINDED TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1110(F). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/MJM)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-23-2014 Set For Trial On Jan-20-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-25-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Ronald Wilson, Tel. 323.365.5013, plaintiff appearing on his own behalf by Court Call;
S. Ashar Ahmed, Esq. of Nossaman LLP, Tel. 415.438.7243, for defendant and moving party.

RULING: The Court allowed arguments. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling with modification. DEFENDANT WESTSIDE COMMUNITY MENTAL HEALTH CENTER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is

Sustained as to the third cause of action without leave to amend because Plaintiff indicated at the hearing he did not want to proceed as to the cause of action. Overruled as to the first, second, and fourth causes of action. For the first cause of action for breach of written contract, a written contract may be pled either in haec verba or according to legal effect. (Twaite v. Allstate Ins. Co. (1989) 216 Cal.App.3d 239, 252.) In this case, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege the legal effect of the written fee agreement; Plaintiff alleges that on November 23, 2010, the parties entered into a written agreement where Plaintiff would perform legal services at a rate of $ 350.00/hour. (Complaint, 3.) For the second cause of action for quantum meruit, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts. Plaintiff may plead inconsistent facts. (Mendoza v. Rast Produce Co., Inc. (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1395, 1402.) Defendant's reliance on Hedging Concepts, Inc. v. First Alliance Mortgage Co. (1996) 41 Cal.App.4th 1410 is inapposite at the pleading stage because there has been no finding of fact that would preclude recovery based on an implied contract. For the third cause of action for account stated, Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts. Plaintiff has not alleged that Defendant agreed that the amount stated is correct or that Defendant promised to pay the stated amount. (CACI no. 373(2) & (3).) For the fourth cause of action for open book account, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts. (CACI no. 372.) An open book account may consist of a single entry reflecting the establishment of an account. (Joslin v. Gertz (1957) 155 Cal.App.2d 62, 66.) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
